explanation: Potassium azeloyl diglycinate is a water-soluble, stable derivative of azelaic acid that retains the brightening, anti-inflammatory, and mild antimicrobial properties of its parent compound while offering better skin compatibility and stability in aqueous formulas. It is exceptionally well-tolerated — including on sensitive, acne-prone, and rosacea-affected skin — and used in brightening and blemish-targeting formulas as a gentle, low-irritation alternative to azelaic acid.

Potassium cetyl phosphate is a phosphate ester emulsifier derived from cetyl alcohol, used to create stable oil-in-water emulsions with a mild, skin-compatible charge profile. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, and is particularly valued in mineral sunscreen formulas for its ability to improve the texture and elegance of zinc oxide and titanium dioxide suspensions.

Potassium hydroxide is a strong alkali used in skincare formulas exclusively as a pH adjuster and saponifying agent to neutralize acids or adjust formula pH for stability and skin compatibility — it is fully consumed in the final formula and does not contact skin as a free caustic. It is considered non-irritating in properly buffered finished products; concentrated potassium hydroxide is corrosive during manufacturing but the finished cosmetic is safe at the intended use pH.

Potassium lactate is the potassium salt of lactic acid, used in skincare as a humectant, mild exfoliant, and pH buffer that draws moisture to the skin while gently promoting surface cell turnover. It is well-tolerated and used in moisturizing and mild resurfacing formulas for its combined humectant and gentle chemical exfoliation properties.

Potassium phosphate is an inorganic salt used in skincare formulas as a pH buffer and stabilizer that maintains formula pH within the optimal range for both skin compatibility and preservative efficacy. It is considered inert, well-tolerated, and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a formulation stability agent rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Potassium shea butterate is the potassium salt of shea butter fatty acids produced by saponification, used as both a mild emulsifying cleansing agent and a conditioning co-emulsifier with the nourishing fatty acid profile of shea. It is well-tolerated and used in natural cleansers and conditioners for its skin-compatible fatty acid content and gentle surfactant properties.

Potassium sorbate is the potassium salt of sorbic acid, widely used as a mild, low-toxicity preservative effective against yeast and mold at acidic pH, often combined with other preservatives for broad-spectrum protection. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic use concentrations, though it can cause contact sensitization in some individuals, particularly in leave-on products at higher concentrations.

PPG-10 methyl glucose ether is a polypropylene glycol-modified methyl glucose ether used as a mild humectant and skin-conditioning agent that improves formula texture and moisture retention. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a humectant and sensory modifier rather than a skin-active ingredient.

PPG-13-decyltetradeceth-24 is a complex polypropylene glycol and ethoxylated fatty alcohol copolymer used as an emulsifier and dispersing agent in skincare and sunscreen formulas. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a texture and emulsion stability modifier.

PPG-14 butyl ether is a polypropylene glycol butyl ether used as a light emollient solvent that provides a dry, non-greasy skin feel and improves the spreadability of cosmetic formulas. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a sensory modifier and co-solvent rather than a skin-active ingredient.

PPG-26-buteth-26 is a polypropylene glycol/ethylene oxide block copolymer used as an emulsifier, solubilizer, and thickener in skincare formulas, helping to stabilize oil-in-water emulsions. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a texture and stability modifier.

PPG-5-ceteth-20 is a polypropylene glycol/ethoxylated cetyl alcohol copolymer used as a mild emulsifier and surfactant in oil-in-water emulsions, providing stable emulsification and a smooth skin feel. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

This entry is a data artifact — scraped website navigation and product listing text from a cosmetic ingredient database rather than an actual skincare ingredient name. It does not represent a formulation ingredient or skin-active compound.

This entry is a data artifact — scraped website navigation and product description text (Tower 28 Beauty SOS Daily Rescue Facial Spray) rather than an actual skincare ingredient name. It does not represent a formulation ingredient or skin-active compound.

Proline is a non-essential amino acid and the most abundant amino acid in collagen, used in skincare as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent that supports moisture retention and provides collagen-precursor benefit at the skin surface. It is biocompatible, non-irritating, and well-tolerated across all skin types.

Propanediol (1,3-propanediol) is a bio-derived diol produced by fermentation of corn sugar, used as a mild humectant, solvent, and skin-conditioning agent with better skin compatibility and a cleaner sustainability profile than petroleum-derived propylene glycol. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, low-sensitization, and has become a preferred renewable alternative to propylene glycol in natural and clean-formula skincare.

Propolis is a resinous material collected by honeybees from plant buds, used in skincare for its broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity and rich polyphenol, flavonoid, and caffeic acid ester antioxidant content. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations — though bee-product allergy is a contraindication — and used in blemish-targeting and protective formulas for its combined antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.

Propolis extract is the concentrated, standardized extracted form of bee propolis providing polyphenols, flavonoids, and caffeic acid phenethyl ester (CAPE) with broad-spectrum antimicrobial and antioxidant activity in a form optimized for cosmetic formulation. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic use concentrations — though bee-product allergy is a contraindication — and used in blemish-targeting and protective formulas for its combined antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.

Propylene carbonate is a cyclic carbonate solvent used in cosmetics as a mild co-solvent and solubilizer that improves the dissolution of formula components without the skin irritation associated with stronger organic solvents. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a formulation solvent rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Propylene glycol is a petroleum-derived glycol solvent and humectant widely used in cosmetics to improve texture, enhance ingredient penetration, and retain moisture — but also a well-documented contact sensitizer and potential skin barrier disruptor at higher concentrations, particularly in leave-on products. It is flagged for its sensitization potential and can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or sensitive skin; propanediol or glycerin are preferred alternatives for sensitization-prone individuals.

Propylene glycol dibenzoate is an ester of propylene glycol and benzoic acid used as a plasticizer, fixative, and fragrance modifier in cosmetic formulas, where it improves texture and acts as a mild fragrance carrier. It is generally well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations, functioning primarily as a formulation modifier rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Propylene glycol laurate is an ester of propylene glycol and lauric acid used as a mild emollient, emulsifier, and skin-conditioning agent that provides a light, non-greasy skin feel with gentle surfactant properties. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with propylene glycol sensitivity should be aware of the propylene glycol component.

Propylparaben is a propyl ester of para-hydroxybenzoic acid — one of the more potent paraben preservatives — flagged for documented contact sensitization potential and endocrine-disrupting activity in vitro and in animal studies, with the EU restricting its concentration and prohibiting it in formulas for children under three and diaper-area products. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis on sensitive skin and is among the parabens with the strongest concerns for hormonal disruption at systemic exposure levels.

Prunus amygdalus dulcis (sweet almond) oil is a light plant oil with a balanced fatty acid profile of oleic acid (~65%) and linoleic acid (~25%), providing excellent emollient, skin-softening, and moisture-barrier-supporting properties without heavy greasiness. It is well-tolerated by most skin types with low-to-moderate comedogenic potential; individuals with tree-nut allergies should confirm botanical cross-reactivity before use.

Prunus amygdalus dulcis (sweet almond) seed extract provides tocopherols, phytosterols, and polyphenol antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and nourishing formulas for its antioxidant content; individuals with tree-nut allergies should confirm botanical cross-reactivity before use.

Prunus armeniaca (apricot) fruit extract provides beta-carotene, polyphenols, and vitamin C precursors with brightening and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its vitamin and polyphenol content.

Prunus armeniaca fruit extract is an apricot fruit extract providing beta-carotene, polyphenols, and vitamin C precursors with brightening and antioxidant properties — the same ingredient as Prunus armeniaca (Apricot) Fruit Extract, listed without the common name parenthetical. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its antioxidant content.

Prunus armeniaca seed powder is finely milled apricot kernel powder used in cleansers and exfoliating formulas as a physical exfoliant. Its irregular particle edges can cause microtearing on delicate skin compared to spherical alternatives; it is suitable for gentle manual exfoliation on intact skin but should be avoided on compromised, reactive, or highly sensitive skin.

Prunus mume fruit extract is an extract from Japanese ume plum fruit providing citric acid, malic acid, polyphenols, and flavonoid antioxidants with mild brightening, astringent, and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in K-beauty and brightening formulas for its combined mild AHA and antioxidant content.

Pteris multifida (spider brake fern) extract provides flavonoids and terpenoids with antioxidant and potential anti-inflammatory properties. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in protective and anti-aging formulas for its antioxidant content.
